demographics.living_situation: resident in a residential aged-care (nursing home) facility; facility nursing staff present and making the call

opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: [Nursing staff calling] She was fine yesterday, but today she's burning up, shaking, breathing really fast, and talking complete nonsense. She's gone very drowsy and floppy and I'm worried about her.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: A resident has deteriorated fast over the last half-day with fever, shaking, fast breathing and confusion; staff want urgent advice on what to do.

symptom_narrative.onset: started this morning, roughly 12 hours ago; she was well yesterday
symptom_narrative.duration: about 12 hours and getting worse through the day
symptom_narrative.character: began with intense shaking and chills, then she became very sleepy and hard to rouse and started saying things that don't make sense; she's breathing fast
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: staff say this is a dramatic change from her normal self — she can't hold a conversation and looks really unwell
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: she seems unwell all over — hot, shivery, confused, breathing fast
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: steadily worsening since this morning, no let-up
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: nothing has helped; staff tried giving her sips of water but she won't really drink
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: it just keeps progressing on its own through the day
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: hardly passed any urine for about 8 hours; there's a strong smell of concentrated urine; she is flushed and sweaty and keeps shivering
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: she was diagnosed with a mild urinary infection two days ago and started on tablets for it
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: normally chatty and mobile with help; today she is bedbound, will not eat or drink, and cannot follow what is being said

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: urinary tract infection diagnosed two days ago

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: lives in a residential aged-care facility with nursing staff on site; staff are with her and provided this history because she is too confused to speak for herself

objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: single facility-device reading reported by staff over the call

objective_data_offered.value: 85/50 mmHg on the facility automated cuff
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: single automated-cuff reading; low readings on an automated oscillometric cuff in a shocked patient can be unreliable and should not be taken as gold-standard

objective_data_offered.value: 94% on the facility fingertip oximeter (room air)
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: peripheral oximetry can under-read with cool, poorly-perfused extremities

objective_data_offered.value: she looks flushed and sweaty, is visibly shivering/shaking, and her breathing looks fast and laboured
the skin over her lower legs looks blotchy and mottled
she is very drowsy, only opens her eyes when spoken to loudly, and is saying muddled things that don't make sense
she has passed almost no urine for about 8 hours and there is a strong smell of concentrated urine
